description: AVAP (API Value Access Protocol) command language for building API logic as composable, bytecode-compiled commands.

parameter_types.variable: Name of a runtime variable stored in conector.variables
parameter_types.value: A literal value or variable reference, resolved at runtime
parameter_types.var: Alias for variable — name of a runtime variable

commands.name: addVar
commands.description: Declares or updates a runtime variable. Performs intelligent type resolution: if varValue is an existing variable name, uses its value; if it is a numeric string, converts to int; otherwise stores as-is.

parameters.name: targetVarName
parameters.description: Name of the variable to create or update

parameters.name: varValue
parameters.description: Value to assign — resolved from variables, numeric strings, or literals

commands.name: addResult
commands.description: Exports a runtime variable to the API response results object. The variable value is copied from conector.variables to conector.results under the same key.

parameters.name: sourceVariable
parameters.description: Name of the variable to export as a result

commands.name: addParam
commands.description: Reads a parameter from the HTTP request (query string, JSON body, or form data) and stores it in a runtime variable. Handles type coercion automatically.

parameters.name: param
parameters.description: Name of the HTTP request parameter to read

parameters.name: variable
parameters.description: Runtime variable name to store the value in

commands.name: if
commands.description: Conditional branch. Compares two values using the given comparator. Executes the true branch if the comparison holds, the false branch (else block) otherwise.

parameters.name: variable
parameters.description: Left-hand side — resolved from conector.variables

parameters.name: variableValue
parameters.description: Right-hand side — resolved from conector.variables or used as literal

commands.name: else
commands.description: Marks the beginning of the false branch of an if block. Must be paired with a preceding if() and a following end().

commands.name: end
commands.description: Closes an if/else or startLoop block. Required to terminate all branching and loop constructs.

commands.name: startLoop
commands.description: Begins a counted loop. Iterates a variable from a start value to an end value (inclusive), incrementing by 1 each iteration.

parameters.name: varName
parameters.description: Loop counter variable name

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Start value (inclusive) — literal or variable reference

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: End value (inclusive) — literal or variable reference

commands.name: endLoop
commands.description: Closes a startLoop block.

commands.name: try
commands.description: Begins an error-protected block. Any runtime error within the block is caught and stored in __last_error__. Execution continues after the exception() handler.

commands.name: exception
commands.description: Catches an error from a preceding try() block. The error message is stored in the given variable and in __last_error__.

parameters.name: error
parameters.description: Variable name to store the caught error message in

commands.name: RequestGet
commands.description: Performs an HTTP GET request. Raises an exception on 4xx/5xx responses (compatible with try/exception). Parses JSON response automatically.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: Request URL — resolved from variable or used as literal

parameters.name: querystring
parameters.description: Query parameters — dict variable or empty

parameters.name: headers
parameters.description: HTTP headers — dict variable or empty

parameters.name: o_result
parameters.description: Variable to store the response body (parsed JSON or text)

commands.name: RequestPost
commands.description: Performs an HTTP POST request. Automatically detects JSON vs form-encoded body. Parses JSON response automatically.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: Request URL

parameters.name: querystring
parameters.description: Query parameters — dict variable or empty

parameters.name: headers
parameters.description: HTTP headers — dict variable or empty

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: Request body — dict (sent as JSON) or string (sent as raw)

parameters.name: o_result
parameters.description: Variable to store the response

commands.example:
AddvariableToJSON(name, Alice, payload)
RequestPost(apiUrl, , , payload, response)
addResult(response)

commands.name: randomString
commands.description: Generates a random string matching a regex pattern with the given length.

parameters.name: Pattern
parameters.description: Regex pattern each character must match (e.g. [A-Za-z0-9])

parameters.name: Length
parameters.description: Number of characters to generate

parameters.name: TargetVariable
parameters.description: Variable to store the generated string

commands.name: encodeSHA256
commands.description: Computes the SHA-256 hex digest of a string variable.

parameters.name: SourceVariable
parameters.description: Variable containing the string to hash

parameters.name: TargetVariable
parameters.description: Variable to store the hex digest

commands.returns: SHA-256 hex string stored in TargetVariable

commands.name: encodeMD5
commands.description: Computes the MD5 hex digest of a string variable. Supports backtick template interpolation (e.g. `prefix_${varName}`).

parameters.name: SourceVariable
parameters.description: Variable or template string to hash

parameters.name: TargetVariable
parameters.description: Variable to store the hex digest

commands.returns: MD5 hex string stored in TargetVariable

commands.name: replace
commands.description: Applies a regex substitution to a string variable.

parameters.name: SourceVariable
parameters.description: Input string variable

parameters.name: rePattern
parameters.description: Regex pattern to match

parameters.name: newValue
parameters.description: Replacement string

parameters.name: TargetVariable
parameters.description: Variable to store the result

commands.name: getRegex
commands.description: Extracts all regex matches from a string and joins them into a single result string.

parameters.name: SourceVariable
parameters.description: Input string variable

parameters.name: rePattern
parameters.description: Regex pattern to match

parameters.name: TargetVariable
parameters.description: Variable to store the joined matches (None if no match)

commands.returns: Joined match string or None stored in TargetVariable

commands.name: ormDirect
commands.description: Executes a raw SQL query against the configured database connection. For SELECT: stores list of row dicts. For INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE: stores rowcount string. Commits automatically unless in sandbox mode.

parameters.name: prompt
parameters.description: SQL query string — variable or literal (backtick strings with ${var} interpolation supported)

parameters.name: TargetVariable
parameters.description: Variable to store query results

commands.returns: List of row dicts (SELECT) or success string (DML) stored in TargetVariable
commands.example:
addVar(sql, SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = 1)
ormDirect(sql, users)
addResult(users)

commands.name: getDateTime
commands.description: Returns the current date/time formatted as a string or timestamp, with optional timezone and time delta offset.

parameters.name: Format
parameters.description: strftime format string (e.g. %Y-%m-%d). Empty string returns Unix timestamp.

parameters.name: TimeDelta
parameters.description: Seconds to add/subtract from current time (0 for no offset)

parameters.name: TimeZone
parameters.description: IANA timezone name (e.g. Europe/Madrid) or empty for local

parameters.name: TargetVariable
parameters.description: Variable to store the result

commands.returns: Formatted datetime string or timestamp stored in TargetVariable

commands.name: getTimeStamp
commands.description: Converts a date string to a Unix timestamp, with optional time delta offset.

parameters.name: DateString
parameters.description: Input date string to parse

parameters.name: Format
parameters.description: strftime format of the input string

parameters.name: TimeDelta
parameters.description: Seconds to add/subtract (0 for none)

parameters.name: TargetVariable
parameters.description: Variable to store the Unix timestamp (float)

commands.name: stampToDatetime
commands.description: Converts a Unix timestamp to a formatted datetime string.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Variable containing the Unix timestamp (float)

parameters.name: Format
parameters.description: strftime format for the output string

parameters.name: TimeDelta
parameters.description: Seconds to add/subtract (0 for none)

parameters.name: TargetVariable
parameters.description: Variable to store the formatted string

commands.returns: Formatted datetime string stored in TargetVariable

commands.name: getQueryParamList
commands.description: Retrieves all query string parameters from the current HTTP request as a list of single-key dicts.

parameters.name: TargetVariable
parameters.description: Variable to store the list of parameter dicts

commands.returns: List of {param: value} dicts stored in TargetVariable

commands.name: getListLen
commands.description: Returns the length of a list variable.

parameters.name: SourceVariable
parameters.description: Variable containing the list

parameters.name: TargetVariable
parameters.description: Variable to store the integer length

commands.name: itemFromList
commands.description: Retrieves a single item from a list by index.

parameters.name: SourceVariable
parameters.description: Variable containing the list

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: Zero-based integer index (literal or variable)

parameters.name: TargetVariable
parameters.description: Variable to store the retrieved item

commands.name: variableToList
commands.description: Appends a value to a list variable. Creates the list if it does not exist.

parameters.name: element
parameters.description: Variable or value to append

parameters.name: TargetVariable
parameters.description: List variable to append to

commands.name: AddvariableToJSON
commands.description: Adds or updates a key-value pair in a dict variable. Creates the dict if it does not exist. Supports backtick template interpolation for both key and value.

parameters.name: Key
parameters.description: Key name — literal, variable reference, or backtick template

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: Value — literal, variable reference, or backtick template

parameters.name: TargetVariable
parameters.description: Dict variable to write to

commands.example:
AddvariableToJSON(name, Alice, payload)
AddvariableToJSON(age, 30, payload)
RequestPost(url, , , payload, response)

commands.name: variableFromJSON
commands.description: Reads a value from a dict variable by key and stores it in a new variable.

parameters.name: SourceVariable
parameters.description: Dict variable to read from

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Key to look up

parameters.name: TargetVariable
parameters.description: Variable to store the extracted value (None if key missing)

commands.returns: Value at key stored in TargetVariable
